# BI-260011: Connect sign-in and sign-up to the GraphQL backend with Google Sign-In

- Backlog Item ID: BI-260011
- Title: Connect sign-in and sign-up to the GraphQL backend with Google Sign-In
- Status: Done
- Persona/Actor: Returning user signing in; new user signing up; Google Workspace user using
  Google SSO; product evaluator using the demo account
- Value statement: For any user starting from `crystord.com`, deliver complete end-to-end
  authentication — email/password, Google SSO, and one-click demo — connected directly to the
  backend GraphQL API.
- Context: The public site is a static Astro build deployed to GitHub Pages. The SaaS app lives
  at `app.crystord.com`. The backend exposes a GraphQL API at `app.crystord.com/graphql` with
  operations for sign-in, sign-up, Google token exchange, and Google client ID lookup. The
  current sign-in form uses a legacy REST endpoint; sign-up and Google SSO are absent from the
  public site.
- Problem statement: Users who want to create an account must navigate to `app.crystord.com`
  directly. Google Sign-In is supported by the backend but not wired up on the frontend. The
  existing sign-in call bypasses the typed GraphQL API. The demo account is inaccessible from
  the public site.
- Expected outcome: A user can sign in, sign up, authenticate with Google, or try the demo
  entirely from `crystord.com` without navigating to `app.crystord.com` first.
- Success metric(s):
  - All four auth paths (sign-in, sign-up, Google SSO, demo) complete end-to-end from the
    public site and result in a redirect to `app.crystord.com/?token=<jwt>`.
  - Unit test coverage is 100% on the two new auth utility modules.
  - No regression on existing e2e and unit test suites.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - Sign-in form calls `query signin(email, password)` on `PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL`; success
    redirects to `app.crystord.com/?token=<jwt>`; failure shows an inline error.
  - `/sign-up` page exists with a form calling `mutation signup(email, password, username)`;
    same success/failure behaviour.
  - Google Sign-In button appears on both auth pages; client ID fetched via
    `query getGoogleClientID`; button hidden gracefully if the query fails.
  - Successful Google auth calls `query signinGoogle(idToken)` and redirects with the JWT.
  - "Try Demo" button on the sign-in form submits with `demo`/`demo` through the sign-in flow.
  - `PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL` is the GraphQL endpoint source; not hardcoded anywhere.
  - All implementations follow the visual token system in `.github/project/branding.md` and
    the component/vertical-slice conventions of ADR-260010.
  - Boundary tests pass; GSI script origin explicitly permitted; no analytics scripts added.
- Non-goals: Implementing the auth backend; implementing the authenticated SaaS shell at
  `app.crystord.com`; social sign-in providers other than Google.
- Constraints: Public site must remain fully static (no Astro SSR); no new npm runtime
  dependencies (vanilla fetch only); GSI script loaded from Google CDN as required by Google.
- Dependencies:
  - GraphQL endpoint at `PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL` must accept cross-origin requests from
    `crystord.com` with `credentials: "include"`.
  - `getGoogleClientID` query must return a valid OAuth client ID registered for
    `crystord.com` as an authorised JavaScript origin.
  - Demo account (`demo` / `demo`) must exist and be active on the backend.
- Risks/assumptions:
  - CORS and credential configuration on the GraphQL endpoint must permit requests from the
    public site origin.
  - Google OAuth client ID must include `crystord.com` in the allowed origins; if not, the
    GSI button will silently fail and the email/password fallback remains.
  - JWT fragment handoff assumes `app.crystord.com` reads and clears the fragment on first
    load. If the app does not implement this, session initialisation fails silently.
- Validation approach:
  - Automated: 100% unit test coverage on `auth-graphql.ts` and `google-auth.ts`; full
    Playwright e2e suite passes.
  - Manual: end-to-end smoke test of all four auth paths against a live backend.
- Priority score: High
- Target window: Next sprint after GraphQL endpoint CORS is confirmed
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260011.md
  - ADR: ADR-260011.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - PR/changeset: n/a
